revision.text:
The Togakushi shrine is a shrine on a mountain near [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano prefecture|Nagano]], [[Japan]]. The Shrine is also part of the in the [[Joshinetsu Kogen National Park]]. The shrine is also known for its natural beauty, especially the approach up the mountain lined with majestic old cedar trees. ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]]
{{commons|Category:Togakushi Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nagano geography stubs]]

The '''Togakushi shrine''' (Japanese: 戸隠神社) is a [[shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano prefecture|Nagano]], [[Japan]]. The Shrine is also part of the in the [[Joshinetsu Kogen National Park]]. There are actually three shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine, (Togakusi houkousya, Togakusi nakasya, and Togakusi okusya respectively) each ca. 2km apart. The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic old [[cedar]] trees.
==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]]
{{commons|Category:Togakushi Shrine}}
{{Nagano-geo-stub}} [[Category:Shinto shrines]] [[ja:戸隠神社]]

The '''Togakushi shrine''' (Japanese: 戸隠神社) is a [[shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano prefecture|Nagano]], [[Japan]]. The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic old [[cedar]] trees.

[[Image:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|200px|The upper shrine]] [[Image:Togakusi nakasya.jpg|thumb|200px|The middle shrine]] [[Image:Togakusi houkousya.jpg|thumb|200px|The lower shrine]] [[Image:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|200px|Sugi lined approach to the upper shrine]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is located within the [[Jōshin'etsu Kōgen National Park]]. There are actually three shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine (Togakushi hokosha, Togakushi chūsha, and Togakushi okusha respectively), each about 2 km apart. The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic old [[Cryptomeria]] trees.

revision.comment: I added pictures caption and links etc... from .jp ;-) please come to Togakushi
revision.text:
[[Image:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|200px|The upper shrine Oku-sya]] [[Image:Togakusi nakasya.jpg|thumb|200px|The middle shrine Chu-sya]] [[Image:Togakusi houkousya.jpg|thumb|200px|The lower shrine Houkou-sya]] [[Image:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|200px|Sugi lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The shrine is located within the [[Jōshin'etsu Kōgen National Park]]. There are actually Five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi houkosha,Hino-miko-sya,Togakushi chūsha,Togakushi okusha and kuzuryu-sya respectively), each about 2 km apart.
The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic old [[Cryptomeria]] trees. and Kuzuryu-sya beside Oku-sya, the establishment than Oku-sya stale, age is unknown. Also the meaning of the name of the Kuzuryu is Nine-Heads-Dragons,Dragon is calling Rain,Togakushi village has abundant spring water from moutains.

*The Houkou-sya(lower shrine) is To a female patron god,God of protect maternity,God of academic life, sewing
*The Hino-miko-sya is God of fire and the performing arts.
*The chu-sya(middle shrine) is God of wisdom.
*The Oku-sya(Upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks.
*The Kuzuryu-sya is God of rain and Landowner God of Togakushi-villages.

In all Five shrine locations,Togakushi-villages.
==External links== * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ Togakushi Shrine official website] in Japanese * [http://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E6%88%B8%E9%9A%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE wikipedia jp Togakushi shrine link] ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] {{commonscat|Togakushi Shrine}} {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[de:Togakushi-Schrein]] [[ja:戸隠神社]]

revision.comment: I added History section and links etc... from .jp ;-)
revision.text:
[[Image:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|200px|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi nakasya.jpg|thumb|200px|The middle shrine Chu-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi houkousya.jpg|thumb|200px|The lower shrine Houkou-sha]] [[Image:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|200px|Planted in the 17 century Sugi lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinjya}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The shrine is located within the [[Jōshin'etsu Kōgen National Park]]. There are actually Five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi houkosha,Hino-miko-sha,Togakushi chūsha,Togakushi okusha and kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart.
The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic old [[Cryptomeria]] trees. and Kuzuryu-sya beside Oku-sha, the establishment than Oku-sha stale, age is unknown. Also the meaning of the name of the Kuzuryu is Nine-Heads-Dragons,Dragon is calling Rain,Togakushi village has abundant spring water from moutains.

*The Houkou-sha(lower shrine) is To a female patron god,God of protect maternity,God of academic life, sewing
*The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts.
*The chu-sha(middle shrine) is God of wisdom.
*The Oku-sha(Upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks.
*The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and Landowner God of Togakushi-villages.

In all Five shrine locations,Togakushi-villages.
Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal[[https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ gosyuin]] to get things possible.
==History== Oku-sha is BC 210, at least one theory, The Emperor AD 684, made the map and Built a temporary building.
AD 849 and being monks "Gakumon" that began in the Oku-sha.
Togakushi shrine mastering flourishing pilgrimage for 8 centuries later, 3000 one temple was located.
33 Cave on Mt. togakushi, pilgrims devoted to prayer in the cave.
Core cave in Oku-sha. Oku-sha building continued into the cave's secret inside the caves.
Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji|Hiei-ｚan]] Temples.
[TogakushisanKansyuinKenkou-ji] was the formal name of the togakushi Temple.
Two major Esoteric Buddhist sects, Singon-sect and Tendai-sect fought against the hegemony Togakushi Temple,Eventually the Singon-sect cause lost the case, left the Togakushi
Togakushi shrine was changed to the shrine from the Temple Ordinance of 19 century Government separation.
It was common for temples and shrines are not split up until 19 century in Japan to be the same place.
Until 19 century was dedicated to [[Avalokiteśvara]].
Togakushi shrine to shrine migrate relatively smoothly from the temple. Seems little damage to the buildings and statues. Also statue was housed in the temple was moved to the nearby temple.
But many separation Decree at the time with the destructive action harsher studied thoroughly, By the privilege of priests until it was increasingly frustrated people.
==External links== * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ Togakushi Shrine official website] in Japanese * [http://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E6%88%B8%E9%9A%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE wikipedia jp Togakushi shrine link] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] {{commonscat|Togakushi Shrine}} {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[de:Togakushi-Schrein]] [[ja:戸隠神社]]

revision.text:
[[Image:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|200px|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi nakasya.jpg|thumb|200px|The middle shrine Chu-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi houkousya.jpg|thumb|200px|The lower shrine Houkou-sha]] [[Image:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|200px|Planted in the 17 century Sugi lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinjya}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]].
The shrine is located within the [[Jōshin'etsu Kōgen National Park]]. There are actually Five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi houkosha,Hino-miko-sha,Togakushi chū-sha,Togakushi oku-sha and kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart.
The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic old [[Cryptomeria]] trees. and Kuzuryu-sha beside Oku-sha, the establishment than Oku-sha stale, age is unknown. Also the meaning of the name of the Kuzuryu is Nine-Heads-Dragons,Dragon is calling Rain,Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.
*The Houkou-sha(lower shrine) is To a female patron god,God of protect maternity,God of academic life, sewing *The chu-sha(middle shrine) is God of wisdom.
*The Oku-sha(Upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks.
*The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and Landowner God of Togakushi-villages.
In all Five shrine locations,Togakushi-villages.
Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal [https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ gosyuin] to get things possible.
==History== Oku-sha is BC 210, at least one theory, The Emperor AD 684, made the map and Built a temporary building.
AD 849 and being monks "Gakumon" that began in the Oku-sha.
Togakushi shrine mastering flourishing pilgrimage for 8 centuries later, 3000 one temple was located.
33 Cave on Mt. togakushi, pilgrims devoted to prayer in the cave.
Core cave in Oku-sha. Oku-sha building continued into the cave's secret inside the caves.
Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji|Hiei-ｚan]] Temples.
(TogakushisanKansyuinKenkou-ji) was the formal name of the togakushi Temple.
Two major Esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Singon-sect]] and [[Tendai|Tendai-sect]] fought against the hegemony Togakushi Temple,Eventually the Singon-sect cause lost the case, left the Togakushi
Togakushi shrine was changed to the shrine from the Temple Ordinance of 19 century Government separation.
It was common for temples and shrines are not split up until 19 century in Japan to be the same place.
Until 19 century was dedicated to [[Avalokiteśvara]].
Togakushi shrine to shrine migrate relatively smoothly from the temple. Seems little damage to the buildings and statues. Also statue was housed in the temple was moved to the nearby temple.
But many separation Decree at the time with the destructive action harsher studied thoroughly, By the privilege of priests until it was increasingly frustrated people.
==External links== * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ Togakushi Shrine official website] in Japanese * [[:ja:%E6%88%B8%E9%9A%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE|Wikipedia jp Togakushi shrine link]] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] {{commonscat|Togakushi Shrine}} {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[de:Togakushi-Schrein]] [[ja:戸隠神社]]

Togakushi shrine mastering flourishing pilgrimage for eight centuries later, 3000 one temple was located.
33 Cave on Mt. togakushi, pilgrims devoted to prayer in the cave.
Core cave in Oku-sha. Oku-sha building continued into the cave's secret inside the caves.
Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji|Hiei-ｚan]] Temples.
(TogakushisanKansyuinKenkou-ji) was the formal name of the togakushi Temple.
Two major Esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Singon-sect]] and [[Tendai|Tendai-sect]] fought against the hegemony Togakushi Temple,Eventually the Singon-sect cause lost the case, left the Togakushi
Togakushi shrine was changed to the shrine from the Temple Ordinance of the 19th century Government separation "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]"," [[Haibutsu kishaku]]","[[Shinto shrine#Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri(Shinto- shrine)]]".
It was common for temples and shrines are not split up until the 19th century in Japan to be the same place.
Until the 19th century was dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
Togakushi shrine to shrine migrate relatively smoothly from the temple. Seems little damage to the buildings and statues. Also statue was housed in the temple was moved to the nearby temple.
But many separation Decree at the time with the destructive action harsher studied thoroughly, By the privilege of priests until it was increasingly frustrated people.
==References== {{reflist|2|refs= {{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans_title = | oclc = 56431036}} }} ==External links== * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ Togakushi Shrine official website] in Japanese * [[:ja:%E6%88%B8%E9%9A%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE|Wikipedia jp Togakushi shrine link]] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] {{commonscat|Togakushi Shrine}} {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] {{Nagano-geo-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[de:Togakushi-Schrein]] [[fr:Togakushi-jinja]] [[ja:戸隠神社]]

revision.text:
[[Image:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|200px|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi nakasya.jpg|thumb|200px|The middle shrine Chu-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi houkousya.jpg|thumb|200px|The lower shrine Houkou-sha]] [[Image:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|200px|Planted in the 17th century Sugi lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinjya}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is located at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) within [[Jōshin'etsu Kōgen National Park]]. Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi houkosha,Hino-miko-sha,Togakushi chū-sha,Togakushi oku-sha and kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart.
The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic old [[Cryptomeria]] trees. and Kuzuryu-sha beside Oku-sha, the establishment than Oku-sha stale, age is unknown. Also the meaning of the name of the Kuzuryu is Nine-Heads-Dragons,Dragon is calling Rain,Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains. *The Houkou-sha(lower shrine) is To a female patron god,God of protect maternity,God of academic life, sewing *The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts. *The chu-sha(middle shrine) is God of wisdom. *The Oku-sha(Upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks. *The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and Landowner God of Togakushi-villages. In all Five shrine locations,Togakushi-villages.
Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal [https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ gosyuin] to get things possible. ==History== Oku-sha is BC 210, at least one theory, The Emperor AD 684, made the map and Built a temporary building.
AD 849 and being monks "Gakumon" that began in the Oku-sha.
Togakushi shrine mastering flourishing pilgrimage for eight centuries later, 3000 one temple was located.
33 Cave on Mt. togakushi, pilgrims devoted to prayer in the cave.
Core cave in Oku-sha. Oku-sha building continued into the cave's secret inside the caves.
Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji|Hiei-ｚan]] Temples.
(TogakushisanKansyuinKenkou-ji) was the formal name of the togakushi Temple.
Two major Esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Singon-sect]] and [[Tendai|Tendai-sect]] fought against the hegemony Togakushi Temple,Eventually the Singon-sect cause lost the case, left the Togakushi
Togakushi shrine was changed to the shrine from the Temple Ordinance of the 19th century Government separation "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]"," [[Haibutsu kishaku]]","[[Shinto shrine#Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri(Shinto- shrine)]]".
It was common for temples and shrines are not split up until the 19th century in Japan to be the same place.
Until the 19th century was dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
Togakushi shrine to shrine migrate relatively smoothly from the temple. Seems little damage to the buildings and statues. Also statue was housed in the temple was moved to the nearby temple.
But many separation Decree at the time with the destructive action harsher studied thoroughly, By the privilege of priests until it was increasingly frustrated people. ==References== {{reflist|2|refs= {{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans_title = | oclc = 56431036}} }} ==External links== * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ Togakushi Shrine official website] in Japanese * [[:ja:戸隠神社|Wikipedia jp Togakushi shrine link]] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] {{commons category|Togakushi Shrine}} {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[de:Togakushi-Schrein]] [[fr:Togakushi-jinja]] [[ja:戸隠神社]]

revision.text:
[[Image:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|200px|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi nakasya.jpg|thumb|200px|The middle shrine Chu-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi houkousya.jpg|thumb|200px|The lower shrine Houkou-sha]] [[Image:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|200px|Planted in the 17th century Sugi lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinjya}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is located at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) within [[Jōshin'etsu Kōgen National Park]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans_title = | oclc = 56431036}} Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi houkosha,Hino-miko-sha,Togakushi chū-sha,Togakushi oku-sha and kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart.
The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic old [[Cryptomeria]] trees. and Kuzuryu-sha beside Oku-sha, the establishment than Oku-sha stale, age is unknown. Also the meaning of the name of the Kuzuryu is Nine-Heads-Dragons,Dragon is calling Rain,Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains. *The Houkou-sha(lower shrine) is To a female patron god,God of protect maternity,God of academic life, sewing *The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts. *The chu-sha(middle shrine) is God of wisdom. *The Oku-sha(Upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks. *The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and Landowner God of Togakushi-villages. In all Five shrine locations,Togakushi-villages.
Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal [https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ gosyuin] to get things possible. ==History== {{cleanup-rewrite}} Oku-sha is BC 210, at least one theory, The Emperor AD 684, made the map and Built a temporary building.
AD 849 and being monks "Gakumon" that began in the Oku-sha.
Togakushi shrine mastering flourishing pilgrimage for eight centuries later, 3000 one temple was located.
33 Cave on Mt. togakushi, pilgrims devoted to prayer in the cave.
Core cave in Oku-sha. Oku-sha building continued into the cave's secret inside the caves.
Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji|Hiei-ｚan]] Temples.
(TogakushisanKansyuinKenkou-ji) was the formal name of the togakushi Temple.
Two major Esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Singon-sect]] and [[Tendai|Tendai-sect]] fought against the hegemony Togakushi Temple,Eventually the Singon-sect cause lost the case, left the Togakushi
Togakushi shrine was changed to the shrine from the Temple Ordinance of the 19th century Government separation "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]"," [[Haibutsu kishaku]]","[[Shinto shrine#Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri(Shinto- shrine)]]".
It was common for temples and shrines are not split up until the 19th century in Japan to be the same place.
Until the 19th century was dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
Togakushi shrine to shrine migrate relatively smoothly from the temple. Seems little damage to the buildings and statues. Also statue was housed in the temple was moved to the nearby temple.
But many separation Decree at the time with the destructive action harsher studied thoroughly, By the privilege of priests until it was increasingly frustrated people. ==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic old [[Cryptomeria]] trees. and Kuzuryu-sha beside Oku-sha, the establishment than Oku-sha stale, age is unknown. Also the meaning of the name of the Kuzuryu is Nine-Heads-Dragons,Dragon is calling Rain,Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains. *The Houkou-sha(lower shrine) is To a female patron god,God of protect maternity,God of academic life, sewing *The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts. *The chu-sha(middle shrine) is God of wisdom. *The Oku-sha(Upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks. *The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and Landowner God of Togakushi-villages. In all Five shrine locations,Togakushi-villages.

AD 849 and being monks "Gakumon" that began in the Oku-sha.
Togakushi shrine mastering flourishing pilgrimage for eight centuries later, 3000 one temple was located.
33 Cave on Mt. togakushi, pilgrims devoted to prayer in the cave.
Core cave in Oku-sha. Oku-sha building continued into the cave's secret inside the caves.
Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji|Hiei-ｚan]] Temples.
(TogakushisanKansyuinKenkou-ji) was the formal name of the togakushi Temple.

Togakushi shrine was changed to the shrine from the Temple Ordinance of the 19th century Government separation "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]"," [[Haibutsu kishaku]]","[[Shinto shrine#Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri(Shinto- shrine)]]".
It was common for temples and shrines are not split up until the 19th century in Japan to be the same place.
Until the 19th century was dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
Togakushi shrine to shrine migrate relatively smoothly from the temple. Seems little damage to the buildings and statues. Also statue was housed in the temple was moved to the nearby temple.

revision.text:
[[Image:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|200px|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi nakasya.jpg|thumb|200px|The middle shrine Chū-sha]] [[Image:Togakusi houkousya.jpg|thumb|200px|The lower shrine Hōkō-sha]] [[Image:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|200px|Planted in the 17th century, the cedar-lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) in [[Jōshin'etsu Kōgen National Park]].<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans_title = | oclc = 56431036}}</ref> Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi Hōkō-sha, Hino-miko-sha, Togakushi Chū-sha, Togakushi Oku-sha and Kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart.
The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic [[Cryptomeria]] trees.
and Kuzuryu-sha beside Oku-sha, the establishment than Oku-sha stale, age is unknown. Kuzuryu means nine-headed dragon. The dragon is calling for rain, and Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.
*The Hōkō-sha (lower shrine) is to a patron goddess, protecting maternity, academic life, and sewing *The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts *The Chū-sha (middle shrine) is God of wisdom *The Oku-sha (upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks *The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and landowner God of Togakushi villages In all Five shrine locations, Togakushi-villages.
Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal [https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ gosyuin] to make things possible.
==History== {{cleanup-rewrite|date=May 2013}} Oku-sha dates to BC 210, in at least one theory.{{citation needed}} The Emperor AD 684, made the map and a temporary building. In AD 849 and monks "Gakumon" began in the Oku-sha.
Togakushi shrine was a pilgrimage sight for eight centuries later, 3000 one temple was located. 33 Cave on Mt. togakushi, pilgrims devoted to prayer in the cave. Core cave in Oku-sha. Oku-sha building continued into the cave's secret inside the caves.
Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples.
Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the togakushi Temple.
Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Singon]] and [[Tendai|Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Singon sect cause lost the case and left Togakushi.
Togakushi shrine was changed to the shrine from the Temple Ordinance of the 19th century government separation "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", "[[Shinto shrine#Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]".
It was common for temples and shrines until the 19th century in Japan to be the same place. Until the 19th century was dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
Togakushi shrine to shrine migrate relatively smoothly from the temple. Seems little damage to the buildings and statues. Statues housed in the temple was moved to the nearby temple.
But many separation decree at the time with the destructive action harsher studied thoroughly. By the privilege of priests until it was increasingly frustrated people.
==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese

The approach to the upper shrine is known for its natural beauty, lined with over 300 majestic [[Cryptomeria]] trees.
and Kuzuryu-sha beside Oku-sha, the establishment than Oku-sha stale, age is unknown. Kuzuryu means nine-headed dragon. The dragon is calling for rain, and Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.

Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the togakushi Temple.
Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Singon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Singon sect cause lost the case and left Togakushi.
Togakushi shrine was changed to the shrine from the Temple Ordinance of the 19th century government separation "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", "[[Shinto shrine#Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]".
It was common for temples and shrines until the 19th century in Japan to be the same place. Until the 19th century was dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".

revision.comment: Added a "site" section. Description of the site
revision.text:
{{cleanup-rewrite|date=May 2013}} [[File:Togakusi houkousya.jpg|thumb|The lower shrine Hōkō-sha]] [[File:160430 Togakushi-jinja Chusha Nagano Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The middle shrine Chū-sha]] [[File:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[File:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|220x220px|Planted in the 17th century, the cedar-lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) in [[Jōshin'etsu Kōgen National Park]].<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans_title = | oclc = 56431036}}</ref> Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi Hōkō-sha, Hino-miko-sha, Togakushi Chū-sha, Togakushi Oku-sha and Kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart.
The approach to the upper shrine is lined with over 300 [[Cryptomeria]] trees.
Kuzuryu means nine-headed dragon. The dragon is calling for rain, and Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.{{fact|date=August 2017}}
*The Hōkō-sha (lower shrine) is to a patron goddess, protecting maternity, academic life, and sewing *The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts *The Chū-sha (middle shrine) is God of wisdom *The Oku-sha (upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks *The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and landowner God of Togakushi villages
Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal gosyuin to make things possible.<ref>https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ</ref>{{clarify|date=August 2017}}
==History== Oku-sha dates to BC 210, in at least one theory.{{citation needed|date=March 2016}} The Emperor AD 684, made the map and a temporary building. In AD 849 and monks "Gakumon" began in the Oku-sha.
Togakushi shrine was a pilgrimage sight for eight centuries later, 3000 one temple was located. 33 Cave on Mt. togakushi, pilgrims devoted to prayer in the cave. Core cave in Oku-sha. Oku-sha building continued into the cave's secret inside the caves.
Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples.
Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the togakushi Temple.
Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Singon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Singon sect cause lost the case and left Togakushi.
Togakushi shrine was changed to the shrine from the Temple Ordinance of the 19th century government separation "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", "[[Shinto shrine#Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]".
It was common for temples and shrines until the 19th century in Japan to be the same place. Until the 19th century was dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
== Site == Upon arrival at Togakusfhi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be crossed by foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well.
== <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://planetyze.com/en/japan/nagano/togakushi-shrine|title=Togakushi Shrine - Nagano Travel Guide {{!}} Planetyze|website=Planetyze|language=en|access-date=2017-08-16}}</ref>See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]

{{Shinto shrine}}
{{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}}

revision.comment: Corrected the existing English and added some information from the Japanese version of the page to make the English page more understandable and coherent.
revision.text: {{cleanup-rewrite|date=May 2013}} [[File:Togakusi houkousya.jpg|thumb|The lower shrine Hōkō-sha]] [[File:160430 Togakushi-jinja Chusha Nagano Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The middle shrine Chū-sha]] [[File:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[File:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|220x220px|Planted in the 17th century, the cedar-lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) in [[Myōkō-Togakushi Renzan National Park]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese |trans-title=| oclc = 56431036}} Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi Hōkō-sha, Hino-miko-sha, Togakushi Chū-sha, Togakushi Oku-sha and Kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart. The approach to the upper shrine is lined with over 300 [[Cryptomeria]] trees. Kuzuryu means nine-headed dragon. The dragon is calling for rain, and Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.{{fact|date=August 2017}} * The Hōkō-sha (lower shrine) is to a patron goddess, protecting maternity, academic life, and sewing * The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts * The Chū-sha (middle shrine) is God of wisdom * The Oku-sha (upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks * The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and landowner God of Togakushi villages Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal gosyuin to make things possible.https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ{{clarify|date=August 2017}} ==History== In one theory{{citation needed|date=March 2016}}, the upper shrine, or Oku-sha, is said to have been first constructed in the 5th year of the Emperor Kogen (210 BC) while Buddhist tradition holds that a monk named Gakumon discovered the Oku-sha area and began the practice of Shugendo there in the 2nd year of the Kasha era (849 AD). According to the [[Nihon-Shoki]], the [[Emperor Tenmu]] had a map of the area made in 684 AD and a temporary building built the following year. Togakushi shrine was a pilgrimage sight during the following eight centuries. Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples. Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the Togakushi Temple. Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Shingon sect lost the battle. Togakushi Temple was changed to a shrine by the Meiji government's Buddhism/Shinto separation initiatives "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", "[[Shinto shrine#Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]" and the 1868 Temple Ordinance. Until that time, it was common in Japan for the same buildings to be used as both temples (Buddhist) and shrines (Shinto). Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]". == Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. == {{Cite web|url=https://planetyze.com/en/japan/nagano/togakushi-shrine|title=Togakushi Shrine - Nagano Travel Guide {{!}} Planetyze|website=Planetyze|language=en|access-date=2017-08-16}}See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

The approach to the upper shrine is lined with over 300 [[Cryptomeria]] trees. Kuzuryu means nine-headed dragon. The dragon is calling for rain, and Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.{{fact|date=August 2017}} * The Hōkō-sha (lower shrine) is to a patron goddess, protecting maternity, academic life, and sewing * The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts * The Chū-sha (middle shrine) is God of wisdom * The Oku-sha (upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks * The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and landowner God of Togakushi villages Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal gosyuin to make things possible.https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ{{clarify|date=August 2017}} ==History== In one theory{{citation needed|date=March 2016}}, the upper shrine, or Oku-sha, is said to have been first constructed in the 5th year of the Emperor Kogen (210 BC) while Buddhist tradition holds that a monk named Gakumon discovered the Oku-sha area and began the practice of Shugendo there in the 2nd year of the Kasha era (849 AD). Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples. Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the Togakushi Temple. Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Shingon sect lost the battle. Togakushi Temple was changed to a shrine by the Meiji government's Buddhism/Shinto separation initiatives "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", and the 1868 Temple Ordinance. Until that time, it was common in Japan for the same buildings to be used as both temples (Buddhist) and shrines (Shinto). Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]". == Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. == {{Cite web|url=https://planetyze.com/en/japan/nagano/togakushi-shrine|title=Togakushi Shrine - Nagano Travel Guide {{!}} Planetyze|website=Planetyze|language=en|access-date=2017-08-16}}See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

{{cleanup-rewrite|date=May 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} | image = Togakusi houkousya.jpg | caption = The lower shrine Hōkō-sha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:160430 Togakushi-jinja Chusha Nagano Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The middle shrine Chū-sha]] [[File:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[File:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|220x220px|Planted in the 17th century, the cedar-lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano (city)]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) in [[Myōkō-Togakushi Renzan National Park]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | trans-title = | oclc = 56431036 | deadurl = yes | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 | df = }} Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi Hōkō-sha, Hino-miko-sha, Togakushi Chū-sha, Togakushi Oku-sha and Kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart. The approach to the upper shrine is lined with over 300 [[Cryptomeria]] trees. Kuzuryu means nine-headed dragon. The dragon is calling for rain, and Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.{{fact|date=August 2017}} * The Hōkō-sha (lower shrine) is to a patron goddess, protecting maternity, academic life, and sewing * The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts * The Chū-sha (middle shrine) is God of wisdom * The Oku-sha (upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks * The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and landowner God of Togakushi villages Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal gosyuin to make things possible.https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ{{clarify|date=August 2017}} ==History== In one theory{{citation needed|date=March 2016}}, the upper shrine, or Oku-sha, is said to have been first constructed in the 5th year of the Emperor Kogen (210 BC) while Buddhist tradition holds that a monk named Gakumon discovered the Oku-sha area and began the practice of Shugendo there in the 2nd year of the Kasha era (849 AD). According to the [[Nihon-Shoki]], the [[Emperor Tenmu]] had a map of the area made in 684 AD and a temporary building built the following year. Togakushi shrine was a pilgrimage sight during the following eight centuries. Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples. Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the Togakushi Temple. Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Shingon sect lost the battle. Togakushi Temple was changed to a shrine by the Meiji government's Buddhism/Shinto separation initiatives "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", and the 1868 Temple Ordinance. Until that time, it was common in Japan for the same buildings to be used as both temples (Buddhist) and shrines (Shinto). Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]". == Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. == {{Cite web|url=https://planetyze.com/en/japan/nagano/togakushi-shrine|title=Togakushi Shrine - Nagano Travel Guide {{!}} Planetyze|website=Planetyze|language=en|access-date=2017-08-16}}See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]". == Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. During winter the paths are closed but snowshoeing may be possible.https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/ == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese * [https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/ Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine] in English {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

The shrine is at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) in [[Myōkō-Togakushi Renzan National Park]].{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | accessdate = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archiveurl = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archivedate = 2007-08-25 }} Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi Hōkō-sha, Hino-miko-sha, Togakushi Chū-sha, Togakushi Oku-sha and Kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart. The approach to the upper shrine is lined with over 300 [[Cryptomeria]] trees, some believed up to 900 years old.https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/ Kuzuryu means nine-headed dragon. The dragon is calling for rain, and Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.{{fact|date=August 2017}} * The Hōkō-sha (lower shrine) is to a patron goddess, protecting maternity, academic life, and sewing * The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts * The Chū-sha (middle shrine) is God of wisdom * The Oku-sha (upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks * The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and landowner God of Togakushi villages Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal gosyuin to make things possible.https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ{{clarify|date=August 2017}} ==History== In one theory{{citation needed|date=March 2016}}, the upper shrine, or Oku-sha, is said to have been first constructed in the 5th year of the Emperor Kogen (210 BC) while Buddhist tradition holds that a monk named Gakumon discovered the Oku-sha area and began the practice of [[Shugendo]] there in the 2nd year of the Kasha era (849 AD). According to the [[Nihon-Shoki]], the [[Emperor Tenmu]] had a map of the area made in 684 AD and a temporary building built the following year. Togakushi shrine was a pilgrimage site during the following eight centuries. Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples. Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the Togakushi Temple. Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Shingon sect lost the battle. Togakushi Temple was changed to a shrine by the Meiji government's Buddhism/Shinto separation initiatives "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", and the 1868 Temple Ordinance. Until that time, it was common in Japan for the same buildings to be used as both temples (Buddhist) and shrines (Shinto). Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]". == Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. During winter the paths are closed but snowshoeing may be possible.https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/ == See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== {{commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese * [https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/ Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine] in English {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]]

Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal gosyuin to make things possible.<ref>https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ</ref>{{clarify|date=August 2017}}

Until that time, it was common in Japan for the same buildings to be used as both temples (Buddhist) and shrines (Shinto). Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
== Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagano Prefecture, Japan}} {{cleanup rewrite|date=May 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} | image = Togakusi houkousya.jpg | caption = The lower shrine Hōkō-sha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:160430 Togakushi-jinja Chusha Nagano Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The middle shrine Chū-sha]] [[File:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[File:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|220x220px|Planted in the 17th century, the cedar-lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano (city)]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) in [[Myōkō-Togakushi Renzan National Park]].<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}</ref> Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi Hōkō-sha, Hino-miko-sha, Togakushi Chū-sha, Togakushi Oku-sha and Kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart.
The approach to the upper shrine is lined with over 300 [[Cryptomeria]] trees, some believed up to 900 years old.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/|title = Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine | Togakushi Ninja |Togakushi Ski Field|date = 17 December 2019}}</ref>
Kuzuryu means nine-headed dragon. The dragon is calling for rain, and Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.{{citation needed|date=August 2017}}
* The Hōkō-sha (lower shrine) is to a patron goddess, protecting maternity, academic life, and sewing * The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts * The Chū-sha (middle shrine) is God of wisdom * The Oku-sha (upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks * The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and landowner God of Togakushi villages
Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal gosyuin to make things possible.<ref>https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ</ref>{{clarify|date=August 2017}}
==History== In one theory{{citation needed|date=March 2016}}, the upper shrine, or ''Oku-sha'', is said to have been first constructed in the 5th year of the Emperor Kogen (210 BC) while Buddhist tradition holds that a monk named Gakumon discovered the Oku-sha area and began the practice of [[Shugendo]] there in the 2nd year of the Kasha era (849 AD). According to the [[Nihon-Shoki]], the [[Emperor Tenmu]] had a map of the area made in 684 AD and a temporary building built the following year.
Togakushi shrine was a pilgrimage site during the following eight centuries. Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples. Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the Togakushi Temple.
Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Shingon sect lost the battle. Togakushi Temple was changed to a shrine by the Meiji government's Buddhism/Shinto separation initiatives "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", and the 1868 Temple Ordinance. Until that time, it was common in Japan for the same buildings to be used as both temples (Buddhist) and shrines (Shinto). Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
== Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. During winter the paths are closed but snowshoeing may be possible.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/|title = Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine | Togakushi Ninja |Togakushi Ski Field|date = 17 December 2019}}</ref>
== See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese * [https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/ Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine] in English

* The Hōkō-sha (lower shrine) is to a patron goddess, protecting maternity, academic life, and sewing * The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts * The Chū-sha (middle shrine) is God of wisdom * The Oku-sha (upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks * The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and landowner God of Togakushi villages
Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal gosyuin to make things possible.<ref>https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ</ref>{{clarify|date=August 2017}}
==History== In one theory{{citation needed|date=March 2016}}, the upper shrine, or ''Oku-sha'', is said to have been first constructed in the 5th year of the Emperor Kogen (210 BC) while Buddhist tradition holds that a monk named Gakumon discovered the Oku-sha area and began the practice of [[Shugendo]] there in the 2nd year of the Kasha era (849 AD). According to the [[Nihon-Shoki]], the [[Emperor Tenmu]] had a map of the area made in 684 AD and a temporary building built the following year.
Togakushi shrine was a pilgrimage site during the following eight centuries. Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples. Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the Togakushi Temple.
Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Shingon sect lost the battle. Togakushi Temple was changed to a shrine by the Meiji government's Buddhism/Shinto separation initiatives "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", and the 1868 Temple Ordinance. Until that time, it was common in Japan for the same buildings to be used as both temples (Buddhist) and shrines (Shinto). Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
== Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. During winter the paths are closed but snowshoeing may be possible.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/|title = Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine | Togakushi Ninja |Togakushi Ski Field|date = 17 December 2019}}</ref>
== See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese * [https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/ Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine] in English

revision.text:
{{sources|date=June 2022}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagano Prefecture, Japan}} {{cleanup rewrite|date=May 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} | image = Togakusi houkousya.jpg | caption = The lower shrine Hōkō-sha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} [[File:160430 Togakushi-jinja Chusha Nagano Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The middle shrine Chū-sha]] [[File:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[File:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|220x220px|Planted in the 17th century, the cedar-lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano (city)]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) in [[Myōkō-Togakushi Renzan National Park]].<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}</ref> Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines, known as the lower, middle, and upper shrine area (Togakushi Hōkō-sha, Hino-miko-sha, Togakushi Chū-sha, Togakushi Oku-sha and Kuzuryu-sha respectively), each area about 2 km apart.
The approach to the upper shrine is lined with over 300 [[Cryptomeria]] trees, some believed up to 900 years old.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/|title = Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine | Togakushi Ninja |Togakushi Ski Field|date = 17 December 2019}}</ref>
Kuzuryu means nine-headed dragon. The dragon is calling for rain, and Togakushi village has abundant spring water from mountains.{{citation needed|date=August 2017}}
* The Hōkō-sha (lower shrine) is to a patron goddess, protecting maternity, academic life, and sewing * The Hino-miko-sha is God of fire and the performing arts * The Chū-sha (middle shrine) is God of wisdom * The Oku-sha (upper shrine) is God's marvelous threw rocks * The Kuzuryu-sha is God of rain and landowner God of Togakushi villages
Around five shrine and pilgrimage certification seal gosyuin to make things possible.<ref>https://www.google.co.jp/search?q=%E5%BE%A1%E6%9C%B1%E5%8D%B0&oe=utf-8&aq=t&rls=org.mozilla:ja:official&hl=ja&client=firefox&um=1&ie=UTF-8&tbm=isch&source=og&sa=N&tab=wi&biw=1154&bih=568&sei=G6JZULLzOOqZmQXVyYDADQ</ref>{{clarify|date=August 2017}}
==History== In one theory{{citation needed|date=March 2016}}, the upper shrine, or ''Oku-sha'', is said to have been first constructed in the 5th year of the Emperor Kogen (210 BC) while Buddhist tradition holds that a monk named Gakumon discovered the Oku-sha area and began the practice of [[Shugendo]] there in the 2nd year of the Kasha era (849 AD). According to the [[Nihon-Shoki]], the [[Emperor Tenmu]] had a map of the area made in 684 AD and a temporary building built the following year.
Togakushi shrine was a pilgrimage site during the following eight centuries. Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples. Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the Togakushi Temple.
Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Shingon sect lost the battle. Togakushi Temple was changed to a shrine by the Meiji government's Buddhism/Shinto separation initiatives "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", and the 1868 Temple Ordinance. Until that time, it was common in Japan for the same buildings to be used as both temples (Buddhist) and shrines (Shinto). Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".
== Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. During winter the paths are closed but snowshoeing may be possible.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/|title = Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine | Togakushi Ninja |Togakushi Ski Field|date = 17 December 2019}}</ref>
== See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese * [https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/ Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine] in English

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nagano Prefecture, Japan}} {{more citations needed|date=June 2022}} {{cleanup rewrite|date=May 2013}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} | image = Togakusi houkousya.jpg | caption = The Lower Shrine, Hōkō-sha | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|44|34|N|138|05|07|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano City]] | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | prefecture = [[Nagano Prefecture]] | leadership = }} [[File:160430 Togakushi-jinja Chusha Nagano Japan01s3.jpg|thumb|The middle shrine Chū-sha]] [[File:Togakusi okusya.jpg|thumb|The upper shrine Oku-sha]] [[File:Tree lined path to the Togakushi shrine1.jpg|thumb|220x220px|Planted in the 17th century, the cedar-lined approach to the upper shrine Oku-sha]] The {{Nihongo|'''Togakushi Shrine'''|戸隠神社|Togakushi Jinja}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Togakushi, Nagano|Togakushi]], [[Nagano (city)|Nagano City]], [[Nagano Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is at the base of [[Mount Togakushi]] ({{Convert|1904|m|ft}}) in [[Myōkō-Togakushi Renzan National Park]].<ref>{{cite encyclopedia | encyclopedia = Dijitaru Daijisen | title = Togakushiyama | url = http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | access-date = 2012-12-04 | year = 2012 | publisher = Shogakukan | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | oclc = 56431036 | url-status = dead | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20070825113418/http://rekishi.jkn21.com/ | archive-date = 2007-08-25 }}</ref>
== Shrines == Togakushi Shrine consists of five shrines that can be visited in any order:
* The Lower Shrine (宝光社, ''Hōkō-sha'') enshrines the deity Ame-no-Uwaharu-no-Mikoto. It is believed to offer blessings related to academic life, [[sewing]], safe [[Childbirth|delivery]], and the protection of women and children.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=戸隠神社 {{!}} 霊山・戸隠山の麓を中心に創建された二千年余りに及ぶ歴史を刻む神社 |url=https://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/english/ |access-date=2024-08-20 |website=www.togakushi-jinja.jp |language=ja}}</ref> * ''Hinomiko-sha'' (火之御子社) enshrines four deities with the main deity being [[Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto]]. There are two 500 year old [[Cryptomeria|Japanese cedars]] known as the "husband and wife cedars" (夫婦の杉, ''Fufu-no-Sugi)'' and a famous [[cherry blossom]] tree known as [[Saigyō|Saigyo]] Zakura. The shrine is believed to offer blessings for marriage, love, fire prevention, and entertainment.<ref name=":0" /> * The Middle Shrine (中社, ''Chū-sha'') has the head office of Togakushi Shrine. It enshrines [[Omoikane|Ame-no-Yagokoro-Omoikane-no-Mikoto]] and has several Japanese cedar trees ranging from 700 to 800 years old. It offers blessings for things such as academic achievement and business prosperity.<ref name=":0" /> * The Upper Shrine (奥社, ''Oku-sha'') enshrines Ame-no-Tachikarao-no-Mikoto. The approach to the shrine is about 2 kilometers long.<ref name=":1" /> The path to the shrine is lined with 400 year old Japanese cedars which are designated as [[Monuments of Japan|Natural Monuments of Japan]].<ref name=":1" /> About halfway through the approach to the shrine, there is a red gate called ''Zuishinmon'' (随神門). The shrine is believed to offer good fortune, good harvests, and victory in sports.<ref name=":1" /> * ''Kuzuryu-sha'' (九頭龍社) literally means "nine-headed dragon shrine." It is dedicated to [[Kuzuryū|Kuzuryu-no-Okami]], the deity of water, rain, and the prevention of [[tooth decay]].<ref name=":1" />
There is a ''[[Shuin|goshuin]]'' stamp available at each of the five shrines.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=戸隠神社 {{!}} 霊山・戸隠山の麓を中心に創建された二千年余りに及ぶ歴史を刻む神社 |url=https://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ |access-date=2024-08-14 |website=www.togakushi-jinja.jp |language=ja}}</ref> Many of the shrines are separated by several kilometers, so personal vehicles and regularly-scheduled buses can be used for transportation between shrines.<ref>{{Cite web |title=アクセス |url=https://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/access/ |access-date=2024-08-20 |website=www.togakushi-jinja.jp |language=ja}}</ref>
It is the [[Sōja shrine]] of [[Kazusa Province]].
==History== In one theory{{citation needed|date=March 2016}}, the upper shrine, or ''Oku-sha'', is said to have been first constructed in the 5th year of the Emperor Kogen (210 BC) while Buddhist tradition holds that a monk named Gakumon discovered the Oku-sha area and began the practice of [[Shugendo]] there in the 2nd year of the Kasha era (849 AD). According to the [[Nihon-Shoki]], the [[Emperor Tenmu]] had a map of the area made in 684 AD and a temporary building built the following year.
Togakushi shrine was a pilgrimage site during the following eight centuries. Its name was ranked with the [[Ise Grand Shrine|Ise-jingu]] Shrine, [[Mount Kōya|Koya-san]] Temple and [[Enryaku-ji]] temples. Togakushisan Kansyuin Kenkou-ji was the formal name of the Togakushi Temple.
Two major esoteric Buddhist sects, [[Shingon Buddhism|Shingon]] and [[Tendai]] fought for the hegemony of Togakushi Temple. Eventually the Shingon sect lost the battle. Togakushi Temple was changed to a shrine by the Meiji government's Buddhism/Shinto separation initiatives "[[Shinbutsu bunri]]", "[[Haibutsu kishaku]]", and the 1868 Temple Ordinance. Until that time, it was common in Japan for the same buildings to be used as both temples (Buddhist) and shrines (Shinto). Until the 19th century, Buddhist activities at the Togakushi Temple were dedicated to "[[Avalokiteśvara]]".{{citation needed|date=July 2022}}
== Site == Upon arrival at Togakushi it is recommended to first visit Oku-sha and then Kuzuryu-sha. It is a 2 kilometer hike from the entrance to the two shrines, however the path leading deep into the mountain can only be taken on foot. Beyond the cedar-lined path, you will be able to see the ''torii'' gate for Oku-sha at the bottom of the mountain and the ''shaden'' main building of the shrine as well. During winter the paths are closed but snowshoeing may be possible.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/|title = Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine | Togakushi Ninja |Togakushi Ski Field|date = 17 December 2019}}</ref>
== See also == * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]] * [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]] * {{ill|Iigaoka Hachimangu|ja|飯香岡八幡宮}} (the other [[Sōja shrine]] of [[Kazusa Province]]) * [[Tamasaki Shrine]] ([[Kazusa Province]] [[Ichinomiya]])
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Togakushi Shrine}} * [http://www.togakushi-jinja.jp/ 戸隠神社 (''Togakushi-jinja'') website] * [http://www.genbu.net/data/sinano/togakusi4_title.htm Togakushi-shrine's Pilgrimage stamp. gensyoushinokioku] in Japanese * [https://naganonow.com/explore/togakushi-village-shrine/ Togakushi Village | Togakushi Shrine] in English
{{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}}
